verdicts.reason: Source slug 'regis-groves' is a character node; TRIGGERS requires event→event. The causal source should be 'refusal-of-regis-groves-to-kneel' (new event node) or the edge should be retyped AGENT_IN if Regis-as-actor is the intent. The quote and tier-2/disputed grounding are correct.

Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:303. MOTIVATES with character-disposition target is correct; Regis's death troubles Tyland's mind but produces no organized action within the unit. Tier-1 accurate.
Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:161. TRIGGERS (death→funeral rites) correctly captures the mandated next beat. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:165. ENABLES not CAUSES is correct; the death creates the vacancy, but the cousins' free decision to attack (via 'argue with swords') is the actual producing act. Tier-1 accurate.
Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:171. ENABLES not CAUSES is correct; the death vacates the seat, but the regents' free choice to appoint Peake is the actual act. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:21. ENABLES is correct; Peake's regency seat is the precondition for his presence and standing to seize the combined office, but his decision to overturn the king's appointments is the actual causing act. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:131/137. MOTIVATES with character-disposition target is correct; the death is the origin of Aegon's lasting psychological trait (dragon-induced rage), not a discrete decision-event. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:93. ENABLES not CAUSES is correct; holding consolidated office makes the Feast possible, but Peake's free decision to decree it is the actual act. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:187. MOTIVATES is correct; Alyn's Stepstones triumph and new admiralty are what make him the obvious candidate the king (speaking Peake's words) taps for the ironmen mission. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:191 ('The trap was neatly set'). MOTIVATES is correct; Peake's enmity/scheming drives the decision to dispatch Alyn on a perilous mission. Tier-2/disputed is appropriate because 'trap' characterization is Gyldayn's interpretive judgment, not stated fact. Surrounding text establishes Peake's intent (S209 'removed the insolent Oakenfist from court') without naming it outright.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:61. SUSPECTED_OF tier-2 is perfect fit. Text explicitly states 'no shred of proof' and frames Peake as 'only truly plausible culprit' if murder occurred. Suspicion grounded in motive and opportunity, never proven — exactly the SUSPECTED_OF pattern. Tier-2 cap enforced correctly.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:69. MOTIVATES with character-disposition target is correct; Gyldayn lays out Peake's presumed motive (need to remarry the king). Tier-2/disputed appropriate because this reasoning sits inside hedged 'if murdered' passage — Gyldayn posits motive without asserting causation as fact.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:36-37. MOTIVATES with character-disposition target is correct. Tier-2/disputed/unattributed correctly tags this as one hedged rumor among several ('Some suggested... Others... countered'); not a firm claim.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:64. MOTIVATES with character-disposition target is correct; narrowed succession field (Rhaena's loss) sharpens Peake's alarm. Tier-1 accurate — Gyldayn narrates this straightforwardly as Peake's concern, not hedged.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:94-95. TRIGGERS is correct; lords' immediate outrage ('Unwin Peake had overreached himself') is the direct undeliberated reaction to the betrothal announcement, the very next beat. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:110-112. CAUSES is correct; text's own 'forced to think again' and 'bowing to the inevitable' language marks real causation — opposition PRODUCES Peake's decision to hold the ball as a fallback. Not a mere precondition (ENABLES). Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:129. SUSPECTED_OF tier-2 is correct. Chronicler explicitly declines to resolve whether Peake caused tragedies ('Were the Hand and his minions responsible...or were they happenstance? In the end it would not matter.'). Suspicion grounded in circumstantial motives and opportunity, never proven. Exactly the SUSPECTED_OF pattern capped tier-2.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:166-168. Role edge PARTICIPATES_IN correctly captures Rhaena's active role presenting Daenaera; part of the marquee moment that breaks Peake's scheme. Tier-1 accurate.
Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:172-173. Role edge ATTENDS correctly marks Daenaera's presence at the ball as a key participant. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:182. MOTIVATES with character-disposition target is correct; the ball's outcome (choosing the 6-year-old over Myrielle) curdles Peake's animus toward the king and twins — a standing disposition that drives later conduct. Tier-1 accurate.
Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p01:15. CAUSES is correct; unmediated disease causation (fever takes his life in two days). Tier-1 accurate.
Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:317. CAUSES is correct; straightforward disease causation (older man succumbs). Tier-1 accurate.
Quote verified fab-the-hooded-hand-21:319 (OCR artifact 'Viserys |' copied verbatim per quoting rule is noted). CAUSES is correct; unmediated disease causation. Tier-1 accurate.

Quote verified fab-war-and-peace-and-cattle-shows-22-p02:20-21. CAUSES is correct; straightforward disease spread to new location. Tier-1 accurate. (Proposal correctly routes this through concept.medical winter-fever node since no umbrella outbreak event exists; appropriate use of existing infrastructure.

new_node_checks.reason: Era targaryen-rule, 132 AC correct (Regis dies during attempted Harrenhal reclamation). Type event.death appropriate. Identity concise and accurate ('witch queen' Alys Rivers context, disputed MEANS of death noted).
Era targaryen-rule, 132 AC correct (follows Corlys Velaryon's death 132 AC day 6 of moon 3). Type event.appointment appropriate. Identity accurate; Peake's resentment at original exclusion is noted.

Era targaryen-rule, 133 AC (moon 9 day 22) correct per text. Type event.death appropriate. Identity accurate; suicide-vs-murder ambiguity clearly marked. Maegor's Holdfast window/spikes details verified.

Era targaryen-rule, 133 AC correct. Type event.incident appropriate (political protest, not battle/assassination attempt). Identity accurate; specific lords (Rowan, Mooton, Tully, Blackwood, Arryn, Stark) named in text. Genuine discrete event with scene.

Era targaryen-rule, 133 AC (moons leading to Maiden's Day) correct. Type event.incident appropriate (rash of injuries/deaths to rival maidens). Identity accurate — text explicitly names 'Maiden's Day curse' (line 129) and lists injuries (Tyshara scandal, Lucinda's nosing, Falena's fall, Buckler drowning). Discrete event hub for cluster of temporally/causally related incidents involving Peake's suspected sabotage. Legitimate graph node, not edge-only, because (1) explicitly named in source, (2) cluster has narrative coherence, (3) downstream edges attach to it (I-E17: SUSPECTED_OF).

summary: 21 CONFIRM / 1 ADJUST / 0 REJECT. Non-CONFIRM: I-E01 (source should be refusal event, not character node regis-groves).
